1.description: (Color online) Evolution of Average Acquirer Announcement Returns Notes. The four panels plot the coefficients 𝐒 S for the vector 𝐘 Y of year dummies corresponding to the CAR regressions reported in Table 3 , as follows: T o p l e f t p a n e l : 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 [ − 3 , + 3 ] 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝐒 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝜖 𝑖 T o p r i g h t p a n e l : 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 [ − 3 , + 3 ] 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝐒 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝚪 ⁢ 𝐗 ′ 𝐢 + 𝜖 𝑖 B o t t o m l e f t p a n e l : 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 [ − 3 , + 3 ] 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝐒 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝚪 ⁢ 𝐗 ′ 𝐢 + 𝐼 𝑛 𝑑 𝐹 𝐸 + 𝜖 𝑖 B o t t o m r i g h t p a n e l : 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 [ − 3 , + 3 ] 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝐒 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝚪 ⁢ 𝐗 ′ 𝐢 + 𝐵 𝑖 𝑑 𝑑 𝑒 𝑟 𝐹 𝐸 + 𝜖 𝑖 . Top left panel : CAR [ − 3 , + 3 ] i = α + S ⁢ Y i ′ + ϵ i Top right panel : CAR [ − 3 , + 3 ] i = α + S ⁢ Y i ′ + Γ ⁢ X i ′ + ϵ i Bottom left panel : CAR [ − 3 , + 3 ] i = α + S ⁢ Y i ′ + Γ ⁢ X i ′ + IndFE + ϵ i Bottom right panel : CAR [ − 3 , + 3 ] i = α + S ⁢ Y i ′ + Γ ⁢ X i ′ + BidderFE + ϵ i . The vector 𝐘 Y includes indicator variables for deal announcement years from 1990 to 2017, with all the 1980s as the omitted category. Thus, coefficients 𝐒 S estimate the change in average acquirer CAR [−3, +3] relative to acquisitions announced during the 1981–1989 period. Grey-shaded areas correspond to a 99% confidence interval.

2.description: (Color online) Evolution of Average Bidder Fixed Effects Notes. The three subfigures plot the coefficients 𝚯 Θ for the vector 𝐘 Y from a regression of estimated bidder fixed effects on year dummies, as follows: T o p l e f t p a n e l : ̂ 𝐵 𝑖 𝑑 𝑑 𝑒 𝑟 𝐹 𝐸 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝚯 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝜖 𝑖 T o p r i g h t p a n e l : ̂ 𝐵 𝑖 𝑑 𝑑 𝑒 𝑟 𝐹 𝐸 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝚯 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝚪 ⁢ 𝐗 ′ 𝐢 + 𝜖 𝑖 B o t t o m l e f t p a n e l ̂ 𝐵 𝑖 𝑑 𝑑 𝑒 𝑟 𝐹 𝐸 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝚯 ⁢ 𝐘 ′ 𝐢 + 𝚪 ⁢ 𝐗 ′ 𝐢 + 𝐼 𝑛 𝑑 𝐹 𝐸 + 𝜖 𝑖 . Top left panel : BidderFE i ^ = α + Θ ⁢ Y i ′ + ϵ i Top right panel : BidderFE i ^ = α + Θ ⁢ Y i ′ + Γ ⁢ X i ′ + ϵ i Bottom left panel BidderFE i ^ = α + Θ ⁢ Y i ′ + Γ ⁢ X i ′ + IndFE + ϵ i . The vector 𝐘 Y includes indicator variables for deal announcement years from 1990 to 2017, with all the 1980s as the omitted category. Thus, coefficients 𝚯 Θ estimate the change in the average bidder fixed effect for all bidders conducting deals in a given year relative to the average bidder fixed effect during the 1981–1989 period. Bidder fixed effects are estimated in a regression specification reported in column (4) of Table 3 . Gray-shaded areas correspond to a 99% confidence interval.

3.description: (Color online) Permutation Analysis: Randomizing Acquirer IDs Notes. The histogram presents the distribution of the coefficient on the Year trend variable in a firm fixed effects specification across the permutations. One thousand permutations of the data are performed: each time the acquiring firm identifiers are randomly shuffled across firms within a given year. The vertical line indicates the coefficient on the Year trend variable in the firm fixed effects specification run on the actual data.

4.description: (Color online) Estimating 𝜃 θ by Bidder Uniqueness Subsamples Notes. This figure plots the estimated synergy-sharing parameter 𝜃 θ and the associated 99% confidence intervals for subsamples according to bidder uniqueness; 𝜃 θ is obtained as one minus the inverse of the slope coefficient 𝛽 β (i.e. 𝜃 = 1 − 1 / 𝛽 θ = 1 − 1 / β ) from the following cross-sectional regression: 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 𝐶 𝑂 𝑀 𝐵 𝑖 = 𝛼 + 𝛽 ⁢ 𝑅 𝐸 𝑆 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 𝑇 ⁢ 𝑖 + 𝜖 𝑖 . CAR COMBi = α + β ⁢ RESCAR T ⁢ i + ϵ i . 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 𝐶 𝑂 𝑀 𝐵 𝑖 CAR COMBi is the weighted average of Target CAR [−41, +3] and Acquirer CAR [−3, +3] with respective market capitalizations (−42 for the target and −4 for the acqurier) as weights; 𝑅 𝐸 𝑆 𝐶 𝐴 𝑅 𝑇 RESCAR T is the target dollar gain obtained from multiplying the Target CAR [−41, +3] by the target market capitalization on day −42 and scaled by the combined market capitalization of the target on day −42 and the bidder on day −4. Regressions are estimated separately for each subsample according to each of the four bidder uniqueness proxies. The top left plot refers to subsamples according to the returns-based proxy for bidder uniqueness ( Uniqueness ( 𝜌 ρ ret) ). The top right plot refers to subsamples according to the sales-correlation-based proxy for bidder uniqueness ( Uniqueness ( 𝜌 ρ sales) ). The bottom left plot refers to subsamples according to the ROA correlation–based proxy for bidder uniqueness ( Uniqueness ( 𝜌 ρ ROA) ). The bottom right plot refers to subsamples according to the text-based proxy for bidder uniqueness ( Uniqueness (HP) ). An empty space indicates that the particular subsample is unpopulated.

5.description: (Color online) Is Bidder Financing Power No Longer a Unique Resource? Notes. The graph plots the incremental R 2 effect of adding a measure of cash flow to annual cross-sectional investment-to- Q regressions run separately for bidders and nonbidders over time. Bidders (solid line) are all firms conducting an acquisition that satisfies our sample selection criteria, as well as any firm that reports positive acquisition CAPEX in its cash flow statement in Compustat in a given year. Nonbidders (dashed line) are all other firms in Compustat in that year.

6.description: (Color online) Are Bidders Falling Behind in Terms of Knowledge Capital? Notes. The graph plots the average percentage of knowledge capital to total capital (R&D stock based on perpetual inventory method divided by the sum of tangible and intangible capital) following Peters and Taylor (2017) separately for bidders and nonbidders over time. Bidders (solid line) are all firms conducting an acquisition that satisfies our sample selection criteria, as well as any firm that reports positive acquisition CAPEX in its cash flow statement in Compustat in a given year. Nonbidders (dashed line) are all other firms in Compustat in that year. Gray-shaded areas correspond to a 99% confidence interval.
